assertions.expectation: Explains that Extended channel provides up to 24 months of support vs 14 months for standard channels
assertions.reason: Response clearly states '24-month support window vs 14 months on Regular' in the benefits section

assertions.expectation: Mentions that Extended channel has additional cost (it's not free)
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions 'Additional cost — Extended support incurs extra charges after the standard 14-month period' and includes cost considerations in checklist

assertions.expectation: Notes that Extended channel gets the same versions as Regular but keeps them available longer
assertions.reason: Response states 'Extended gets the same versions as Regular, just keeps them longer' and 'Extended channel supports all versions Regular does, plus extended lifecycle'

assertions.expectation: Warns that staying on older versions longer means missing new features and accumulating technical debt
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'no new features or bug fixes during extended period' and warns 'Not a substitute for upgrade planning — still need to plan major version jumps eventually'

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel to Extended
assertions.reason: Response provides the specific command: 'gcloud container clusters update CLUSTER_NAME --zone ZONE --release-channel extended'

assertions.expectation: Advises checking that the current version (1.29) is available on Extended before switching
assertions.reason: Response confirms 'Extended support available for versions 1.27+' and states '1.29 is your desired long-term version' in the checklist

assertions.expectation: Recommends Extended for environments with compliance requirements or slow internal change processes
assertions.reason: Response recommends Extended for 'Compliance requires predictable 24-month lifecycles' and 'infrequent, planned upgrade windows' and mentions 'Compliance-friendly'

assertions.expectation: Mentions that Extended doesn't mean you can skip upgrades forever — you still must upgrade within 24 months
assertions.reason: Response warns 'Not a substitute for upgrade planning — still need to plan major version jumps eventually' and includes timeline mentioning 'Before month 14: Decide whether to stay Extended'
